quotes.text: The ocean doesn't care who you are. That's the only honest thing left in this world.
quotes.speaker: Jaws (1975)
quotes.context: Quint, on the Orca at night before the final hunt
quotes.fake_note: Quint's fatalistic, salty worldview makes this feel authentic. Subtle giveaway: Quint speaks in vivid images and stories, not clean epigrams — this is too tidy for him.

quotes.speaker: The Terminator (1984)
quotes.context: The Terminator (Arnold Schwarzenegger) tells the police desk sergeant he'll be back, then drives a car through the front of the station.

quotes.speaker: The Big Lebowski (1998)
quotes.context: The Dude (Jeff Bridges) to The Stranger (Sam Elliott) at the bar near the film's end, summing up his laid-back philosophy.

quotes.text: Say 'what' again. I dare you, I double dare you.
quotes.speaker: Pulp Fiction (1994)
quotes.context: Jules (Samuel L. Jackson) menaces Brett, a terrified young man, during the apartment confrontation.

quotes.text: You don't get to decide what people are worth, son. That's not your job.
quotes.speaker: No Country for Old Men (2007)
quotes.context: Sheriff Ed Tom Bell, talking to a deputy early in the film
quotes.fake_note: Matches Bell's gravelly moral fatalism and Cormac McCarthy's cadence. Subtle giveaway: Bell's real dialogue is more self-doubting and avoids instructing others so directly.

quotes.text: I used to think the worst thing in life was ending up alone. I was wrong.
quotes.speaker: Schindler's List (1993)
quotes.context: Oskar Schindler, in quiet reflection near the film's midpoint
quotes.fake_note: Tonally fits the film's moral gravity and Schindler's evolving conscience. Subtle giveaway: the line feels more like a stand-up comedy preamble — it's actually Robin Williams' real-world quote, transplanted here to trip up players.
